name: Structured JSON request logging via Pino

sections.context: The Todos API (app.js, Express) currently has no request logging at all — grep for pino/logger/durationMs/requestId returns nothing, and package.json only depends on express. Coord coord-ac249ad6 requires that every API request emits exactly one structured JSON log line via Pino, carrying method, path, status, durationMs, and requestId, with a unique requestId per request and no body/header logging. This is needed for operational visibility (one auditable line per request) and to establish the requestId convention for future cross-cutting concerns.
sections.approach: Add a single Pino-backed request-logging middleware mounted at the top of the Express stack in app.js, right after express.json() and before any route. The middleware will: (1) assign a requestId per request using crypto.randomUUID() (unique per request, no external dependency, no header requirement), attaching it to req.id; (2) start a high-resolution timer (process.hrtime.bigint or Date.now) on request entry; (3) log exactly once on res.on('finish') using pino, including method (req.method), path (req.path/req.url minus query), status (res.statusCode), durationMs (computed from the timer), and requestId (req.id). Exactly-one-line is guaranteed because 'finish' fires exactly once per request. pino is added to package.json dependencies. The logger instance is exported so tests can capture its output. We deliberately avoid pino-http/auto-request-logging to keep full control of the one-line invariant and the exact fields, and we do not log bodies or headers per the non-goals.

alternatives.name: Use pino-http middleware
alternatives.rejected_because: It logs on its own hooks and adds extra/autogenerated fields; harder to guarantee exactly one line and the exact field set, and risks duplicate lines if combined with a custom handler. A hand-rolled finish-handler gives precise control over the one-line invariant and field set.

alternatives.name: Use morgan with JSON format
alternatives.rejected_because: Violates the explicit constraint to use Pino for logging.

alternatives.name: Read requestId from an inbound header (e.g. x-request-id)
alternatives.rejected_because: The success criterion requires each request to get a unique requestId with no header dependency or trust assumptions; crypto.randomUUID() guarantees uniqueness without header input and stays within non-goals (we are not logging headers, only generating an id).

steps.title: Add Pino request-logging middleware to app.js
steps.description: Add pino to package.json dependencies and install it. Create/export a Pino logger instance and mount a request-logging middleware in app.js after express.json() and before the routes. The middleware must assign a unique requestId via crypto.randomUUID() to req.id, start a timer on entry, and emit exactly ONE log line on res.on('finish') containing method, path (route path without query string), status, durationMs, and requestId. Must not log request/response bodies or headers. Ensure the finish handler is idempotent so exactly one line is emitted even under error/aborted paths. Export the logger so tests can capture output.

steps.title: Add node --test coverage for request logging behavior
steps.description: Add a test file run via `node --test` (npm test already maps to it) that exercises the existing routes (GET /todos, POST /todos, DELETE /todos/:id incl. 400/404) and asserts: (1) each request produces exactly one valid JSON log line; (2) each line contains all required fields (method, path, status, durationMs, requestId) with correct values; (3) requestId differs per request (uniqueness). Capture Pino output via a custom writable destination stream injected through the exported logger (e.g. a logger factory that accepts a stream), so no extra test dependencies (no supertest) are required. Assert the no-body/no-header non-goal holds (line keys contain only the allowed fields).

risks: res.on('finish') must fire exactly once — guard against double-logging if any error middleware or abort path also triggers logging; add an emitted-flag guard.
durationMs must be non-negative and meaningful; use process.hrtime.bigint (or Date.now) and convert to integer ms.
path should be the route path without query string to avoid leaking query/body-adjacent data; strip query before logging.
If pino writes asynchronously to stdout by default, tests must capture output via a synchronous in-memory writable stream passed to the logger to avoid flaky timing.
Adding pino changes the dependency tree — ensure package-lock.json is updated so npm ci / install reproduces.
acceptance: Every API request (all routes incl. error responses) emits exactly one valid JSON log line via Pino.
Each log line contains method, path, status, durationMs, and requestId with correct values.
Each request receives a unique requestId (no two requests share one).
No request/response bodies and no headers appear in any log line.
`npm test` (node --test) passes and includes automated coverage verifying the one-line-per-request invariant, the field set, and requestId uniqueness.
Existing routes (GET/POST/DELETE /todos) behavior and status codes are unchanged.
